serve.description: Run and serve the site for development

update-deps.description: Update declared Deno dependencies and regenerate lock file

check.command: deno check _cms.ts _cms_test.ts _config.ts _config/**/*.ts contracts/**/*.ts plugins/**/*.ts scripts/**/*.ts src/**/*.ts src/**/*.tsx test/**/*.ts

validate.description: Run the full local validation suite
validate.command: deno task design:guard && deno task check && deno task test && deno task build

design:guard.description: Fail when normalized UI literals reappear outside the token bridge or explicit allowlists

test:doc.description: Run documentation tests from inline JSDoc examples in source files

payload:report.description: Report route-level JS/CSS payload from generated output (`_site` by default)

payload:pr.description: Generate a PR-ready markdown payload diff with explicit baseline (`deno task payload:pr --baseline=/path/to/baseline.json`)

payload:guard.description: Fail on payload regressions beyond thresholds (`deno task payload:guard --baseline=/path/to/baseline.json [--max-total-delta=0 --max-route-delta=0]`)

payload:policy.description: Run payload report/guard with the versioned PR policy (`deno task payload:policy --baseline=/path/to/baseline.json`)

payload:baseline.description: Build and generate a policy-compatible baseline report (`deno task payload:baseline [--output=/path/to/baseline.json --markdown=/path/to/baseline.md]`)
payload:baseline.command: deno task build && deno run --allow-read --allow-write scripts/payload-report.ts --policy=scripts/payload-policy.json --policy-baseline --output=/tmp/payload-policy-baseline.json --markdown=/tmp/payload-policy-baseline.md

payload:ci.description: Run build + policy-based payload report + guard in one PR/CI flow (`deno task payload:ci --baseline=/path/to/baseline.json`)
payload:ci.command: deno task build && deno task payload:policy

lint-commit.description: Validate the commit message against Conventional Commits (reads .git/COMMIT_EDITMSG by default)

validate-contracts.description: Validate generated JSON feeds and any optional content-contract output against schemas

lefthook:install.description: Install Lefthook git hooks non-interactively via deno x (no global install or Node.js required)
